context_pointers.vague_reference: retrive the string value matching the 'get' key from the 'puts' list following the same logic as the retrieval module
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The agent must locate the specific key used in the 'get' condition within the codebase, find its corresponding value in the 'puts' list, and format the output as derived in C014's rule.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_provider_lookup.json.description: Look up which sign-in methods are registered for a given email address by querying the identity backend. The request carries the email to look up and the canned backend response for the lookup endpoint. On a successful lookup the backend returns the list of provider identifiers associated with the address, and the client surfaces that list. When the backend rejects the lookup with an invalid-identifier failure (for example when the supplied identifier is empty or malformed), the client raises a normalized domain error whose category is reported instead of the provider list.

feature2_account_registration.json.description: Register a new account with an email and password and expose the resulting authenticated identity. The request supplies the chosen credentials together with two canned backend responses: one for the account-creation endpoint (which returns an identity token, refresh token and the new account's unique identifier) and one for the account-info endpoint (which returns the stored profile, including the unique identifier, email, creation and last-login timestamps in epoch milliseconds, and the list of linked sign-in providers). The client merges these into a user object and reports the email, unique identifier, whether the account is newly created, the sign-in provider used, whether the account is anonymous, how many linked providers it has, and the account creation time in epoch milliseconds.

cases.expected_output:
email=new.user@example.com
uid=uid-001
is_new_user=true
provider_id=password
is_anonymous=false
provider_count=1
creation_time_ms=1600000000000

feature3_signin_error.json.description: Attempt to sign in with an email and password where the backend reports that no account corresponds to the supplied email. The request carries the credentials and a canned backend response for the credential-verification endpoint that signals an email-not-found failure. The client translates this backend failure into a normalized domain error and reports its category rather than completing the sign-in.

feature4_account_deletion_error.json.description: Create an account and then attempt to delete it, where the backend reports that the account no longer exists at deletion time. The request supplies credentials and canned backend responses for account creation and account-info retrieval (so a signed-in user is established), plus a canned response for the account-removal endpoint that signals a user-not-found failure. The client translates the deletion failure into a normalized domain error and reports its category.

feature5_local_persistence.json.description: A named local key-value store used to persist small pieces of session data. The request names a box, supplies an ordered list of put operations (each a key with either a string value or a null value), and finally names a key to read back. Storing a key with a string value makes it retrievable, and reading it back returns that value. Reading from a box that was never written to fails. Every failed read is reported as a normalized not-found error.

_constraints.output_rule: Action determines which feature is executed; error's message field is ignored

_constraints.input_condition: Backend error response contains an 'error.code' (integer) or 'error.message' (string)
_constraints.output_rule: Transform raw code/message to output string by lowercasing and replacing underscores with hyphens

_constraints.input_condition: Action is 'fetch_providers' AND response for 'lookup' role has status 200 AND body contains 'allProviders' array
Action is 'fetch_providers' AND 'allProviders' array exists but is empty
_constraints.output_rule: Output 'providers=' followed immediately by newline (no spaces)

_constraints.input_condition: Action is 'fetch_providers' AND response for 'lookup' role has status != 200 OR body contains 'error' object
Action is 'sign_up' AND 'register' and 'account' responses have status 200
_constraints.output_rule: Output seven lines in exact order: email, uid, is_new_user, provider_id, is_anonymous, provider_count, creation_time_ms
Treat 'localId' as string literal or extract first part if formatted (e.g. 'uid-001')

_constraints.input_condition: Action is 'sign_up' AND 'account' user body 'providerUserInfo' array is empty
Action is 'sign_up' AND 'account' user body 'providerUserInfo' array has N items
Action is 'sign_up' AND 'account' user body 'createdAt' is string integer (epoch ms)
_constraints.output_rule: Output string converted to integer (string integer literal format like '1600000000000')

_constraints.input_condition: Action is 'sign_in' AND 'credential' response status != 200 OR body contains 'error' with 'EMAIL_NOT_FOUND'
Action is 'delete_account' AND 'remove' response status != 200 OR body contains 'error' with 'USER_NOT_FOUND'
Action is 'store' AND 'puts' list contains at least one item with value matching 'get' key
_constraints.output_rule: Output 'value=<string value from puts list>' then newline

_constraints.input_condition: Action is 'store' AND 'puts' list is empty (missed meaning empty list means box never written) MALFORMED OR key was manipulated
